title: Grievance Redressal
tagline: A transparent, fair, and prompt system for addressing student concerns.
overview: NSEC has established a robust Grievance Redressal System in compliance with UGC regulations to address academic, administrative, and personal grievances of students.
The system ensures confidentiality, impartiality, and timely resolution of all complaints filed by students or their parents.
Complaints can be submitted online through the UGC portal, via email, or physically at the Student Affairs office.
The institution takes zero tolerance approach to any form of discrimination, harassment, or academic malpractice.

process.title: Submit Complaint
process.description: Student submits a written or online grievance to the Grievance Redressal Cell or via the UGC portal.

process.title: Acknowledgement
process.description: The cell acknowledges receipt of the complaint within 3 working days and assigns a reference number.

process.title: Investigation
process.description: The committee reviews the complaint, collects evidence, and may call for a hearing with both parties.

process.title: Resolution
process.description: A resolution is communicated to the student within 30 working days. Appeals can be escalated if unsatisfied.

categories: Discrimination based on caste, religion, or gender

online_portals.name: UGC Grievance Portal
online_portals.description: National online portal for student grievances against institutions

online_portals.name: AICTE Grievance Portal
online_portals.description: AICTE's dedicated portal for technical education grievances

online_portals.name: PG Portal (Govt. of India)
online_portals.description: Central Government's Public Grievance redressal portal

contact.name: Grievance Redressal Cell
contact.address: Netaji Subhash Engineering College, Garia, Kolkata - 700152
